qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Authentication bypass when OAuth2 not enforced by NRF
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a default OAuth2 configuration issue in the NRF/UDR registration flow (context.go, nrf_service.go, router_auth_check.go). The advisory describes a missing return statement after a 404 response in a specific path-validation handler (HandleApplicationDataInfluenceDataSubsToNotifySubscriptionIdDelete in api_datarepository.go). These are distinct root causes in different functions.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes a missing return statement after an HTTP 404 response in HandleApplicationDataInfluenceDataSubsToNotifySubscriptionIdDelete (api_datarepository.go) in the free5gc UDR service, which allows deletion of Traffic Influence Subscriptions via an invalid influenceId path segment. The only qualifying finding ('authentication_bypass_oauth2_disabled') addresses a different root cause (OAuth2 not enforced by default in NRF configuration) in different functions (context.go, nrf_service.go, router_auth_check.go).
evidence: Advisory root cause: missing return after c.String(http.StatusNotFound, ...) in HandleApplicationDataInfluenceDataSubsToNotifySubscriptionIdDelete in api_datarepository.go
authentication_bypass_oauth2_disabled root cause: OAuth2Required defaults to false, AuthorizationCheck returns nil in context.go — different function, different file, different mechanism
